name: todoist-blade-mcp
title: Todoist
description: Todoist task management — projects, sections, tasks, labels, comments, incremental sync, batch commands, filter queries
tagline: Todoist tasks with dual API and incremental sync
author: groupthink-dev

env.name: TODOIST_WRITE_ENABLED
env.description: Enable write operations (create, update, complete, delete, batch)

setup.help_text: Generate an API token at Todoist → Settings → Integrations → Developer.
setup.blurb: Connect to Todoist for task management with dual REST v2 + Sync v9 APIs, batch commands, and incremental sync.

highlights.text: Tasks — create, update, complete, delete with full metadata
Projects — hierarchical projects with sections and nesting
Comments — task and project comments for context
Incremental sync — Sync v9 API for efficient delta updates
Batch commands — multiple operations in a single API call

readme:
### What it does

Todoist gives your AI agent full access to Todoist’s task management platform — projects, sections, tasks, labels, comments, and filter queries. Built on both the REST v2 and Sync v9 APIs for maximum efficiency.

Your agent can list projects, create tasks with due dates and labels, add comments, complete items, and run Todoist filter queries to find exactly the tasks you need.

### Dual API architecture

This plugin uses two Todoist APIs strategically:

- **REST v2** for simple CRUD operations — clean, predictable, one resource per call
- **Sync v9** for incremental sync and batch commands — fetch only what changed since the last sync, execute multiple operations in a single round trip

The Sync API’s incremental model is especially efficient for agents that poll task state repeatedly. After the initial sync, subsequent calls transfer only deltas.

### Safety model

Read operations work immediately — list projects, get tasks, search with filters, read comments.

Write operations (create, update, complete, delete, batch) require `TODOIST_WRITE_ENABLED=true`. Disabled by default, so your agent can browse your task database freely without risk of accidental modifications.

### Tool coverage

| Category | Capabilities |
|----------|--------------|
| Tasks | Create, read, update, complete, delete, filter queries |
| Projects | List, create, sections, hierarchy |
| Labels | List, create, assign |
| Comments | Task and project comments |
| Sync | Incremental delta sync |
| Batch | Multiple commands in one call |

scenarios.label: Triage inbox and organise into projects
scenarios.body: List tasks in the inbox, assign each to the appropriate project with labels and due dates, then add sections to projects that need better structure.

scenarios.label: Run a filter query and batch-update results
scenarios.body: Query tasks using Todoist filter syntax to find overdue items, then use batch commands to reschedule them with updated due dates in a single API call.

scenarios.label: Create a project with structured tasks
scenarios.body: Create a new project, add sections for phases or categories, then populate each section with tasks including due dates, labels, and priority levels.
